content:
{
    "improvements": [
        {
            "id": "IMP-001",
            "description": "Resolve [ASSUMPTION-3] (audit primitive_id for model.invoke.complete) before Sprint 1 begins, not during it. Add a concrete decision matrix and required envelope schema bump path (1.1.0→1.2.0) as a pre-Sprint-1 task with explicit owner and deadline.",
            "rationale": "This is foundational to Sprint 1's typed-error/audit work and blocks AC-1.7. Riding 'whichever primitive is the calling context' creates ambiguous audit semantics that will undermine M1 verification queries (§6.3). M1 is the document's headline invariant; ambiguity in its data source is a critical gap.",
            "location": "§4.4, §13 [ASSUMPTION-3], TR2",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.9
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-002",
            "description": "Add an explicit secrets-management and key-isolation specification for the smoke fleet, including key scope (read-only/limited budget), rotation cadence, blast-radius if leaked, and what to do when secrets are absent (graceful skip vs hard fail).",
            "rationale": "TR5 names the risk but the workflow in §9.1 references SMOKE_FLEET_*_KEY secrets without specifying how they differ from production keys, what happens on missing secrets, or any incident-response procedure. For a weekly cron with `issues: write` and auto-commit, secrets handling deserves first-class spec, not a one-line risk row.",
            "location": "§9.1, TR5",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-003",
            "description": "Specify probe-gate behavior for high-concurrency invocation explicitly: thundering-herd prevention when many callers miss cache simultaneously, single-flight semantics under flock, and behavior when probe lock acquisition itself times out.",
            "rationale": "§4.2.3 mentions flock for read-modify-write but doesn't address the 12-simultaneous-BB-calls case beyond 'cache amortizes.' If 12 callers cache-miss together, do they all probe, or does one probe and 11 wait? Lock acquisition timeout is unspecified. This is exactly the failure mode TR4 warns about and needs a concrete algorithm.",
            "location": "§4.2.2, §4.2.3, TR4",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-004",
            "description": "Define the schema migration mechanics for model-config.yaml v1.x → v2.0.0 concretely: migration script location, idempotency, dry-run mode, behavior when both schemas coexist during rollout, and rollback procedure if v2.0.0 load fails.",
            "rationale": "§3.1 bumps schema_version to 2.0.0 and §10 NFR-BC-3 mentions 'migration helper (mirrors cycle-099 sprint-1E.a)' but no concrete spec exists. TR3 acknowledges the risk. A schema bump touching the SoT for all model resolution without a documented migration path is a high-blast-radius gap.",
            "location": "§3.1, §3.2, NFR-BC-3, TR3",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-005",
            "description": "Add a concrete specification for the operator-visible header's behavior under degraded outputs: max line length, truncation rules when N is large (e.g., 12 models), how it renders in non-PR contexts (CLI, logs), and i18n/encoding for unicode markers (✓/✗).",
            "rationale": "§4.3 pins format but doesn't address scaling. With smoke fleet probing many (provider, model) pairs, a single-line header can exceed terminal/PR-comment width. Unicode ✓/✗ may not render in all CI log contexts. M7 verification depends on operators actually seeing this — rendering edge cases matter.",
            "location": "§4.3",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.75
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-006",
            "description": "Specify the contract test corpus for AC-4.4a (kill-switch shim coverage) more concretely: enumerate the legacy adapter's code paths that must be covered, define 'bit-equivalent behavior' precisely (stdout/stderr/exit-code/audit-event tuple), and set a coverage percentage threshold.",
            "rationale": "§8.2 step 2 says 'every code path in the archived legacy adapter has a test' but for 1018 LOC, this is unbounded. Without an enumerated path inventory and equivalence definition, the cycle-103 deletion gate is unverifiable. This is the linchpin of the quarantine-vs-delete decision.",
            "location": "§8.2, AC-4.4a, §11.1 Sprint 4",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.85
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-007",
            "description": "Add a regression-detection algorithm spec for false-positive controls beyond 'two-consecutive-failure': specify behavior for new models added mid-window, transient 429s during smoke run, missing data points (workflow skipped/failed), and provider maintenance windows.",
            "rationale": "§9.3 covers the basic threshold but real-world cron-based detection has many edge cases (sparse data, schema changes, fleet expansion). M5 alerting fidelity depends on this. R4 is acknowledged but the mitigation is thin.",
            "location": "§9.3, R4",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-008",
            "description": "Define a concrete contract for how the tier_groups → capability_classes derivation works at config load time (Sprint 3), including: which is canonical when both are present, error semantics on inconsistency, and the deprecation path for tier_groups.",
            "rationale": "[ASSUMPTION-1] and TR7 acknowledge this is a two-surfaces-one-truth risk, but §5 doesn't specify the derivation algorithm. Operators with existing tier_groups customizations need to know what happens. This affects every existing caller and is a silent-breakage risk class similar to the original cycle-102 motivation.",
            "location": "§3.1, §5.2, [ASSUMPTION-1], TR7",
            "priority": "HIGH",
            "confidence": 0.8
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-009",
            "description": "Specify probe-cache behavior across multiple repo checkouts / parallel CI runners that share no .run/ directory: how does each runner build its own cache, are there cold-start probe storms, and what is the SLO for first-invocation latency in cold-cache scenarios?",
            "rationale": "§4.2.1 places the cache at `.run/model-probe-cache/` (per-repo). In CI matrix builds with N parallel runners, each starts cold; that's N×providers probes per CI run. M6 (<2s probe latency) compounded across cold caches in CI may violate aggregate latency budgets and add real cost.",
            "location": "§4.2.1, M6",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.75
        },
        {
            "id": "IMP-010",
            "description": "Add an explicit observability/debugging surface specification: how does an operator diagnose 'why did this call route to model X?' Include a debug/trace mode for resolver, structured logging of class→fallback-chain decisions, and a CLI introspection command (e.g., `model-resolver explain <class>`).",
            "rationale": "Capability-class indirection adds a routing layer that operators didn't have to reason about before. When the system silently picks fallback[2], operators need a way to ask 'why?' beyond querying audit logs post-hoc. This is essential for adoption confidence and cuts MTTR for the next class of issues. Currently absent from the SDD.",
            "location": "§5 (new subsection), §10 (new NFR-Obs)",
            "priority": "MEDIUM",
            "confidence": 0.8
        }
    ],
    "summary": "10 improvements identified, 6 HIGH priority, 4 MEDIUM priority. Focus areas: unresolved assumptions blocking Sprint 1 (IMP-001), concrete migration/quarantine mechanics (IMP-004, IMP-006), concurrency/scaling edge cases in probe-gate and smoke-fleet (IMP-003, IMP-007, IMP-009), and operator-facing surfaces (IMP-005, IMP-010)."
}
